messages.text: Morning approached, and Belmont held more news than sleep. The arrival of letters had changed fear into relief almost too quickly.

messages.name: Antonio
messages.text: The papers announce that my ships are safe. My wealth is restored, though I had already released myself from expecting it.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Then let gratitude make you wiser, not merely richer. Fortune returns, but it does not always explain why.
Bassanio stood beside her, grateful and humbled. The ring was secure again on his hand, heavier now because he understood it better.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: No pressure, no judge, no friend shall draw it from me again.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Good. Let that resolve last longer than the first.
Gratiano made the same promise to Nerissa with comic terror, and she accepted because laughter can release anger when truth has been admitted.

messages.name: Gratiano
messages.text: While I live, I will fear no thing so much as keeping safe Nerissa's ring.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: Fear helps where memory fails. I accept.
Jessica and Lorenzo received their future in a document created from Shylock's punishment. They were grateful, but the source of their wealth remained complicated.

messages.name: Jessica
messages.text: We gain security from my father's sentence. I cannot rejoice without feeling the cost.

messages.name: Lorenzo
messages.text: Then we will use it with care. A hard gift should teach gentle living.
Antonio watched the young couples and said little. He had been rescued by law, mercy, friendship, and the wife his friend had just married.

messages.name: Antonio
messages.text: I came to court ready for death. I leave with ships, friends, and more mercy than I deserved.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Mercy is not a merchant account. It does not balance neatly, but it may still restore what justice alone would break.
The story did not erase its wounds. Shylock had lost more than money; Antonio had faced the knife; Portia had needed disguise to enter power.
Yet the future opened. Bassanio and Portia, Gratiano and Nerissa, Lorenzo and Jessica all stood inside a peace made from trials.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: Let the morning find us better keepers of bonds, rings, and words.
Belmont answered with music. Venice would return to commerce, but those who survived the bond knew that strict contracts need human mercy.
The last judgment belonged not to the court but to the life after it: whether gratitude could become action, and wealth could become care.
No one proposed another trial. They had all endured enough judgment for one night, and the future needed ordinary acts more than speeches.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Let every document be read in daylight. At night we too easily mistake relief for understanding.
Antonio accepted the advice. Wealth could restore credit and ships, but it could not release him from thinking about the hatred that almost killed him.

messages.name: Antonio
messages.text: I am grateful for life. I must learn what to do with a life returned by another person's courage.
Bassanio and Gratiano kept their rings, not as decorations but as small contracts renewed by embarrassment. Their future depended on remembering the lesson.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Then let mercy become a habit, not a speech saved for courts. That is how peace may last.
The couples remained imperfect, which made the ending livable. They had not escaped law, money, prejudice, or desire; they had survived their collision.

messages.name: Lorenzo
messages.text: Let music announce what papers cannot: that the future is open, but not innocent.
By daylight, every bond looked different. Some were legal, some marital, some chosen by friendship, and all required care beyond words.
The ending is comic because marriages hold, ships return, and music plays. It is serious because every happiness has passed through someone else's pain.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Let us keep the comedy honest. A restored house should remember the storm outside it.
That memory is what gives the final harmony weight.
